question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET request to /api/v1/contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes with Accept-Encoding: gzip and stack=true&memory=true&storage=true, triggering a full EVM re-execution of a complex transaction, causing sustained CPU/memory exhaustion on the server?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the gzip-only enforcement by sending Accept-Encoding: xgzip or Accept-Encoding: notgzip, since the check uses contains('gzip') substring matching, allowing uncompressed large responses that cause network saturation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user consume the single global opcodeRateLimitBucket token (default 1 req/sec) every second, effectively denying all other users access to the opcodes endpoint for the entire duration of the attack?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-client throttling in throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest(), where the single global opcodeRateLimitBucket is shared across all clients, to monopolize the endpoint and deny service to legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that race past the opcodeRateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check, since the opcodeRateLimitBucket is built without SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (unlike gasLimitBucket), allowing multiple simultaneous EVM re-executions that exhaust server resources?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set stack=true (the default), memory=true, and storage=true simultaneously to maximize the EVM re-execution cost and response payload size, causing the server to allocate unbounded memory for a single request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user omit the Accept-Encoding header entirely, causing Spring MVC to throw a MissingRequestHeaderException (400) before the controller code runs, bypassing the throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest() call and allowing unlimited unauthenticated probing of the endpoint without consuming throttle tokens?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user send an extremely long Accept-Encoding header value (e.g., megabytes of data containing 'gzip'), causing the toLowerCase() call to allocate a large string in memory, leading to memory pressure or GC pauses on the server?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transactionIdOrHash path variable with URL-encoded path separators (e.g., %2F, %5C) to attempt path traversal or route confusion in the Spring MVC dispatcher, potentially reaching unintended handler mappings?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transactionIdOrHash value that is valid per the TransactionIdOrHashParameter regex but references a transaction belonging to another user, causing the endpoint to replay and expose that other user's full EVM execution trace including stack, memory, and storage contents?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate any historical transaction hash and retrieve its full opcode trace including storage slot values, effectively reading private contract state at the time of that transaction's execution without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user request opcodes for a transaction that involves a contract holding sensitive business logic, and by enabling stack=true&memory=true&storage=true, reconstruct the contract's internal algorithm and private data from the full execution trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that properties.isEnabled() is the sole access gate (no authentication, no authorization) to access the opcodes endpoint whenever it is enabled, with no identity verification whatsoever?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid transactionIdOrHash for a known high-gas transaction (e.g., a complex DeFi operation), forcing the server to re-execute the entire EVM computation, consuming CPU for the full duration of the original transaction's gas limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the throttle check (throttleManager.throttleOpcodeRequest()) occurs after validateAcceptEncodingHeader(), meaning that requests with invalid Accept-Encoding headers never consume a throttle token, allowing unlimited invalid requests to probe the endpoint without rate-limit consequences?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Accept-Encoding: gzip;q=0,gzip to pass the contains('gzip') check while signaling to intermediate proxies that gzip is not preferred, causing the proxy to decompress the response and forward it uncompressed, negating the bandwidth protection intent?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transactionIdOrHash path variable with maximum-length numeric fields (e.g., shard=9999999999, realm=9999999999, num=9999999999) that pass the regex but cause EntityId.of() to throw an exception, and does the resulting error response leak internal stack trace details?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TransactionIdParameter with seconds=Long.MAX_VALUE (19 digits, allowed by the regex \\d{1,19}) causing Instant.ofEpochSecond() to throw an ArithmeticException or DateTimeException, and does the error response expose internal exception details?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transactionIdOrHash that is a valid 64-hex-character hash but does not exist in the database, causing the EntityNotFoundException message 'Contract transaction hash not found: ...' to be returned, leaking the fact that the hash lookup mechanism and internal repository structure exist?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid TransactionIdParameter that does not exist in the database, causing the EntityNotFoundException message 'Transaction not found: ...' to be returned, confirming the existence or non-existence of specific transaction IDs (oracle/enumeration attack)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any request body size limit or query parameter length limit on the stack, memory, and storage boolean parameters to send malformed non-boolean values that cause Spring type conversion errors, and do those errors leak internal framework details?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests with invalid transactionIdOrHash values (triggering InvalidParametersException before EVM execution), consuming server thread pool resources without triggering the opcodeRateLimitBucket throttle, causing thread exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodeRateLimitBucket uses a greedy refill strategy (refillGreedy) to burst-consume the single token immediately after each refill, maintaining a sustained 1 req/sec load that keeps the EVM re-execution pipeline continuously busy?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with stack=true (default) for a transaction that executed a contract with a very deep call stack, causing the OpcodeContext to accumulate an unbounded list of Opcode objects in memory, leading to OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with memory=true for a transaction that used maximum EVM memory (e.g., MSTORE to high offsets), causing the memory trace to be serialized into a massive response payload that exhausts server heap during JSON serialization?
